Name: gpu_kernel_opt
Title: Optimizing Machine Learning Inference on Commodity GPUs through Kernel-Level Throughput Engineering
Short Hypothesis: Custom kernel fusion and optimized tensor core occupancy can significantly improve the inference latency of machine learning models on commodity GPUs, leading to measurable, hardware-faithful speedups.
Related Work: Existing research has focused on optimizing machine learning inference through model compression, sparsity, and layer-skipping. However, few studies have explored the potential of kernel-level throughput engineering and hardware-faithful evaluation. This proposal builds upon the idea of optimizing machine learning inference on commodity GPUs, but with a focus on custom kernel fusion and tensor core occupancy.
Abstract: This proposal investigates the potential of custom kernel fusion and optimized tensor core occupancy to improve the inference latency of machine learning models on commodity GPUs. Through a combination of kernel-level throughput engineering and hardware-faithful evaluation, we aim to achieve measurable speedups and advance the state-of-the-art in machine learning inference. Our approach will involve designing and implementing custom kernels, optimizing tensor core occupancy, and evaluating the resulting performance improvements using hardware-faithful metrics such as latency, throughput, and energy per token.
Experiments: Design and implement custom kernels for popular machine learning models, focusing on fusion of compute-intensive operations and optimized memory access patterns.
Investigate the impact of tensor core occupancy on arithmetic intensity and develop strategies to optimize occupancy for improved performance.
Evaluate the performance of custom kernels and optimized tensor core occupancy using hardware-faithful metrics, including latency, throughput, and energy per token.
Compare the results to existing state-of-the-art models and optimization techniques, highlighting the benefits and limitations of our approach.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of designing and implementing custom kernels may limit the applicability of our approach to a wide range of machine learning models.
The potential for improved performance may be limited by the underlying hardware architecture and the efficiency of the GPU architecture.
The evaluation metrics used may not capture all aspects of performance, and additional metrics may be necessary to fully understand the benefits and limitations of our approach.
